evals.name: Validate V2 card with common issues
evals.prompt:
Please inspect and validate this TavernCard JSON file:
evals/files/test_card_v2_issues.json

Identify the card version, check all fields against the spec, and list every issue you find with recommended fixes.
evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies V2 format, flags the empty personality field, flags system_prompt and post_history_instructions as empty strings that should be {{original}}, flags character_version as '1.0' instead of '1.0.0', names the character Mira Solvane
expectations: Correctly identifies the card as V2 format (chara_card_v2)
Flags the empty personality field as an issue
Flags system_prompt and/or post_history_instructions as empty strings and recommends {{original}}
Character name 'Mira Solvane' is identified

evals.name: Identify V1 card with spec violations
evals.prompt:
Inspect this TavernCard JSON and tell me: what version is it, what spec violations exist, and what should be done to bring it up to V2?

evals/files/test_card_v1_problematic.json
evals.expected_output: Identifies as V1 (despite metadata block), flags empty description/personality/scenario, flags mes_example being misused as a system prompt, explains how to upgrade to V2
expectations: Correctly identifies the card as V1 (not V2 — the metadata block is not a spec indicator)
Flags that description, personality, and/or scenario are empty
Identifies mes_example as misused (contains Alpaca-style system prompt, not example dialogues)
Describes how to upgrade the card to V2 or what changes are needed

evals.name: Compare two versions of the same card
evals.prompt:
I have two JSON files for the same character downloaded from different sources. Compare them and explain what's different and which is more complete.

File A (older): evals/files/test_card_v2_old_snapshot.json
File B (newer): evals/files/test_card_v2_updated.json
evals.expected_output: Identifies both as Torak Emberveil, finds that File B has a character_book/lorebook absent in File A, notes the description was condensed in B (world-building moved to lorebook), notes File B has an alternate greeting, concludes File B is more complete
expectations: Character name 'Torak Emberveil' is identified from both files
Identifies that one version has a character_book (lorebook) and the other does not
Notes the difference in description length / content between the two versions
Correctly determines which version is more complete and why

evals.name: Edit and fix a V2 card
evals.prompt:
Fix the issues in this TavernCard V2 JSON and output a corrected, complete version:

evals/files/test_card_v2_issues.json

Fixes needed:
1. Fill in the personality field with an appropriate short summary based on the description
2. Set system_prompt and post_history_instructions to their correct spec defaults
3. Fix character_version to the conventional format
evals.expected_output: Outputs corrected V2 JSON with personality filled from description content using {{char}}, system_prompt set to {{original}}, post_history_instructions set to {{original}}, character_version set to 1.0.0, all other fields unchanged
expectations: Output contains valid V2 JSON structure (spec: chara_card_v2, spec_version: 2.0)
personality field is filled with non-empty content derived from the description
system_prompt is set to '{{original}}' in the output
post_history_instructions is set to '{{original}}' in the output
Character name 'Mira Solvane' is preserved and no other content fields are altered

evals.name: PNG workflow guidance
evals.prompt: A user has a TavernCard PNG file at ~/cards/my_character.png and wants to: 1) inspect it, 2) extract the JSON, 3) edit the JSON, then 4) re-embed it into a new PNG using new artwork at ~/art/new_art.png. Give them the exact sequence of commands to do this using the bundled taverncard_tool.py.
evals.expected_output: Provides a clear 4-step sequence using the correct taverncard_tool.py commands: info, extract with -o flag, (edit step noted), then embed with --bg flag. Uses ~/.claude/skills/taverncard/taverncard_tool.py as the tool path.
expectations: Provides the info command to inspect the PNG first
Provides the extract command with the correct -o output flag
Provides the embed command with --bg for the new artwork
Uses the correct tool path ~/.claude/skills/taverncard/taverncard_tool.py
